task: I’ve got a soccer practice coming up where I want to fix a few things from my last game, but I don’t want to overthink it. Can you help me make a simple plan for what to focus on before, during, and after practice so I can actually improve next time?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a simple **before / during / after** plan you can actually use without overthinking it.

## 1) Before practice
- Pick **1–2 things** from the last game to improve
  - Example: first touch, passing speed, defending, communication, finishing
- Decide your **main focus**
  - Ask: “What will help me most next game?”
- Set a simple goal
  - Example: “I’ll stay active off the ball and make quicker passes.”

## 2) During practice
- Focus on your chosen skill, not everything
- Pay attention to **one cue at a time**
  - Example:
    - first touch: “soft touch”
    - passing: “open up”
    - defense: “stay low”
- If you make a mistake, reset fast and keep going
- Listen for coach feedback and try it right away

## 3) After practice
- Write down:
  - what you worked on
  - what felt better
  - what still needs work
- Pick **one thing** to carry into the next practice or game
- Keep it short so it’s easy to remember

### Super simple version
- **Before:** choose 1–2 things
- **During:** focus on one cue
- **After:** note what improved and what to do next

If you want, I can also turn this into a **soccer-specific version** for things like **defending, passing, shooting, or fitness**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is basically the right idea. I just want it a little more soccer-specific and not so generic.

Like, if my last game problem was mostly **spacing / passing / not reacting fast enough**, what would the simple before-during-after plan look like for that? Keep it short. I don’t want a huge list.

turns.agent_response:
Yep — here’s a short soccer-specific version for **spacing / passing / reacting faster**:

### Before
- Remind yourself: **“Get open early, pass simple, move again.”**
- Pick one focus: **find space quicker** or **release the ball faster**

### During
- **Check your spacing** before the ball comes to you
- **Play simple passes** instead of forcing tough ones
- **React right away** after you pass: move, press, or support

### After
- Ask: **Did I get open faster?**
- Ask: **Were my passes simple and accurate?**
- Pick **one thing to fix next time**

If you want, I can make this even shorter into a **3-line game-day reminder** you can memorize fast.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s closer. Still want it a little more game-real though.

Before: remind myself to **scan early** and **move before the ball gets stuck**.
During: **check shoulder, find space, one-touch if it’s there**, and after I pass, **don’t just stand there**.
After: quick note — **did I move enough / pass fast enough / react fast enough?**

That’s the kind of short thing I’d actually use.

turns.agent_response:
That works — here’s a clean version in that style:

### Before
- **Scan early**
- **Move before the ball gets stuck**

### During
- **Check shoulder**
- **Find space**
- **One-touch if it’s there**
- After you pass: **don’t stand there — move again**

### After
- Quick check:
  - **Did I move enough?**
  - **Did I pass fast enough?**
  - **Did I react fast enough?**

If you want, I can turn that into a **one-line reminder** you could say to yourself before practice.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s it. Short enough to actually use, and not weirdly generic.

I can work with that.
